TS-39.title: Fix $TRANSCRIPT shell interpolation in Python heredoc
TS-39.acceptance_criteria: Line 46 passes TRANSCRIPT as env var to python3. Line 49 reads os.environ["TRANSCRIPT"] instead of shell interpolation. No "$TRANSCRIPT" in Python heredoc block. bash -n passes. Python AST parse succeeds.
TS-39.design_section:
## Client Integration in Renderer

A ~25-line Python function inserted into `tmux-claude-status` before the existing bridge-file read. The script uses an unquoted heredoc (`<< PYEOF`) with shell variable interpolation. The fix passes $TRANSCRIPT via environment variable instead of direct interpolation to eliminate shell injection risk.

TS-40.title: Add @app.error(401) JSON handler to server
TS-40.acceptance_criteria: server.py contains @app.error(401) handler that sets content_type to application/json and returns {"error": "invalid_or_missing_api_key"}. Handler placed before @app.error(404). test_error_handlers_registered asserts 401. New unit test for 401 handler. WSGI test asserts Content-Type. All tests pass.
TS-40.design_section:
## Interface / API Design

### Authentication

When `--api-key-file` is provided:
- `/quota` requires `X-API-Key` header matching the file contents
- Comparison uses `hmac.compare_digest()` (timing-safe)
- Missing or wrong key returns 401
- `/health` is exempt

**Auth failure (401):**
```json
{"error": "invalid_or_missing_api_key"}
```

All responses are `Content-Type: application/json`.
